---
title: "Conceptual API"
sidebar: api
---

This API section describes the proposed user-facing concepts. It is not generated API documentation and should not be treated as a promise that every snippet runs today.

## Main User Operations

```python
inventory = inv.Inventory.from_yaml("inventory.yaml")

patch = patch.update_attrs(data_source_id="XX.GARDEN..RAW")

patch = patch.add_inventory_attrs(inventory)
patch = patch.add_inventory_coords(inventory, coords=("x", "y", "z", "label"))
```

## Model Responsibilities

| Concept | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `Inventory` | Top-level metadata catalog and serialized manifest boundary. |
| `Network` | Organizational grouping borrowed from FDSN-style metadata. |
| `FiberArray` | Durable fiber-optic observing identity. |
| `Acquisition` | Interrogator configuration and channel-to-distance context. |
| `OpticalPath` | Optical-distance path with component, geometry, coupling, and annotation tracks. |
| `PatchAttrs` | Patch-local metadata plus `data_source_id` for inventory lookup. |

## Design Rule

Inventory context is copied onto a patch only by explicit user action. That keeps patches lightweight and makes metadata provenance easier to inspect.
